why_not_ignore_user_config: --ignore-user-config bypasses the invalid service_tier, but also bypasses TSF project trust and [windows] sandbox = "elevated".
The failed worker saw a read-only sandbox despite --sandbox workspace-write.
Normal shell could write the fixture directory, and TSF mission/approval checks passed.
why_service_tier_override: Current user config contains service_tier = "flex", which previously failed during runtime.
Local help accepts -c service_tier=null with --sandbox workspace-write.
This preserves user config while overriding the known bad service-tier key without editing config.
approval_mode_flag_available: local help exposes --full-auto but no separate named approval-mode flag
full_auto_reason: Do not expand beyond the narrower config-preserving service-tier override until that path is tried or disproven.
temporary_sanitized_config_reason: A command-line service_tier override is narrower than creating or swapping config files.
worker_retry_cap: one foreground fixture-only codex exec invocation maximum
